claims.claim: Circuit Tracks has exactly two dedicated MIDI tracks (MIDI 1 and MIDI 2) that drive external gear and support all the same sequencer functions as the synth tracks but have no internal sound engine.
Factory-default MIDI channels are Synth 1 = Ch 1, Synth 2 = Ch 2, MIDI 1 = Ch 3, MIDI 2 = Ch 4, and all four Drum tracks share Ch 10.

MIDI channel 16 is reserved for the Project (global project-level CCs and project program-change) and cannot be assigned to a track; tracks may be reassigned only to channels 1-15, and no two tracks may share a channel.
Setup View is entered by holding Shift and pressing Save, and exited by pressing Play; MIDI channel assignment, clock Rx/Tx, and analogue sync rate are all set there.

Circuit Tracks stores eight MIDI templates; you enter Template View by selecting a MIDI track and pressing the Preset button, and the eight templates appear as the top row of blue pads (selected pad lights white).
Each MIDI track can use a different template, and by default all eight templates are identical, mapping the eight Macro knobs to CC1, CC2, CC5, CC11, CC12, CC13, CC71 and CC74 respectively.

MIDI templates can only be created/edited/backed-up in Novation Components, where you set each control's start and end value (anticlockwise/clockwise limits) and choose unipolar or bipolar polarity; polarity affects only the LED behaviour, not the transmitted MIDI data.
Recorded MIDI-template CC automation is transmitted to both the MIDI Out DIN and the USB port, unlike parameter automation on other tracks which is sent to USB only.

On a MIDI track the Macro knobs in Mixer/FX View control the two rear-panel analogue audio Inputs (level, pan, reverb/delay sends), not MIDI; the recommended setup is sending MIDI out to an external synth and routing that synth's audio back into Inputs 1 and 2.

The internal tempo range is 40-240 BPM (default 120) and internal tempo is integer-only, adjusted with Macro 1 in Tempo View; when slaved to external clock CT follows any rate from 30-300 BPM including fractional tempos.
Tap Tempo is done by holding Shift and tapping the Tempo/Swing button; it needs at least three taps and then averages the last five taps.
Swing is adjusted with Macro 2 in Tempo View over a range of 20 to 80 (default 50), affecting the timing of even-numbered off-beat steps.
When Clock Rx is OFF the unit uses internal clock only; when Clock Rx is ON it is in AUTO mode and locks to a valid external MIDI clock at MIDI In or USB, falling back to internal if none is present.
When Clock Tx is ON, Circuit Tracks acts as clock master and transmits MIDI Clock out of both the USB and MIDI Out connectors.
Rx and Tx can be toggled independently for four data categories via Setup View pads 25-32: Note (green), CC (orange), Program Change (purple) and Clock (pale blue); all default to ON.
If Circuit Tracks is slaved and the external clock stops or goes out of range, it stops playing and displays SYN until Play is pressed.
The rear-panel analogue Sync Out emits a 5 V pulse at a selectable rate of 1, 2, 4, 8 or 24 ppqn (default 2 ppqn), and Swing is not applied to this analogue clock output.
Circuit Tracks does not support Ableton Link natively; synchronisation is via MIDI clock (as master or slave) or analogue sync only.
Program Change on Ch 16 with value 0-63 changes the whole Project instantly, while value 64-127 queues the Project change to a pattern boundary.

By default, selecting a new pattern while playing queues it so the current pattern finishes first (its pad flashes fast); holding Shift while selecting switches immediately but continues at the equivalent step for timing continuity.

Circuit Tracks has 16 Scene pads on the Mixer page, each storing a snapshot of the currently-playing patterns/chains; a queued Scene flashes green and takes effect at the end of the currently-playing Drum 1 pattern without losing sync.
Mute is performed in Mixer View (button 11) using Row-1 pads (one per track, dims when muted); there is no dedicated Solo function.
When a track is muted, its sequencer step pads become inactive and can instead be used to play that track's notes/chords or drum hits live in real time over the unmuted tracks.

The dedicated Master Filter knob is a DJ-style filter over the entire output including both external audio inputs, with a centre detent for no filtering; anticlockwise is low-pass and clockwise is high-pass.
The FX View (button 12) provides 16 delay presets and 8 reverb presets with per-track send amounts set by the Macro knobs, and delay time is synced to the project BPM.

Scale and root note (16 scales) are set in Scales View (button 9) and can both be changed during playback; changing the keynote transposes existing sequenced notes non-destructively, substituting out-of-scale notes by about a semitone.
Pressing Play while the sequencer is running restarts the pattern from Step 1 by default; holding Shift while pressing Play instead continues from the step where it was stopped.
The metronome/click is toggled with Shift + Clear (quarter-note tick on all outputs) and its level is set with Macro 5 in Tempo View.
Main outputs are two 1/4-inch TS jacks (L/Mono, R) at a maximum line level of +5.3 dBu; with nothing plugged into the R socket, L/Mono carries a summed mono mix.
The USB-C port carries MIDI, power and firmware only and does not carry audio, so audio to a PA or amp must come from the 1/4-inch jack outputs.

The MIDI Thru port can be reconfigured in Advanced Setup View (hold Shift while powering on) via the Duplicate button to act as a second MIDI Out, allowing control of two external devices that lack their own thru ports.
Master-filter frequency is controllable over MIDI as CC74 on Ch 16 (0-63 low-pass, 64 off, 65-127 high-pass) and its resonance as CC71 on Ch 16.

Circuit Tracks has no focus/master-channel mode for external MIDI control, so each MIDI destination must be addressed on its own channel, which the Sound on Sound review notes as a workflow limitation.

verdicts.note: User Guide 'The MIDI Tracks' section: 'The two MIDI tracks behave just like the Synth tracks and support all the same sequencer functions... They do not control an internal synth engine but are intended to control external equipment or software via MIDI.' Note and CC data transmitted just like synth tracks. Exact match.
User Guide Setup View / MIDI Channels table: Synth 1=1, Synth 2=2, MIDI 1=3, MIDI 2=4, Drums 1-4=10. Verbatim in the factory-default MIDI channels table.

User Guide: 'Each track... can be set to any of MIDI channels 1-15. Channel 16 is reserved for the Project.' And boldface 'Important: No two tracks can transmit on the same MIDI channel.' (The four drum tracks collectively use one shared channel, per the same section.)

User Guide: Setup View 'is entered by holding down Shift and pressing Save, and exited by pressing Play.' Settings listed include MIDI channel assignment, MIDI I/O config (Rx/Tx incl. Clock), external/analogue clock rate (ppqn). All three cited items confirmed.

User Guide 'Selecting Templates': 'Select a MIDI track and press Preset 14 to access Template View. Eight MIDI templates are stored within Circuit Tracks, represented by the top row of blue pads... Select a new template by pressing a pad; it lights white.' Exact match.

User Guide 'Default Templates': 'The eight default MIDI templates are identical' and 'Each track can use a different template.' Default CC table: Macro 1=CC1, 2=CC2, 3=CC5, 4=CC11, 5=CC12, 6=CC13, 7=CC71, 8=CC74. Exact match.

User Guide: templates 'can be edited and backed up in Components Editor' (Key Features also lists creating MIDI track templates in Components); on-device you can only select. Control range: start value=fully anticlockwise output, end value=fully clockwise. Polarity: 'This only affects the response of the LED... and does not affect the transmitted messages.' Exact match.

User Guide: 'automated MIDI template controls will send data to the MIDI Out DIN port as well as the USB port, unlike parameters on the other tracks, which are only sent to the USB port.' Exact match.

User Guide: on MIDI tracks the Macros 'act on the analogue audio signal applied at the two audio Inputs on the rear panel... recommended workflow is to send MIDI data... to your external synth... while routing the audio outputs of that device back into Circuit Tracks' audio inputs.' Volume, pan, delay/reverb sends. Exact match.

User Guide Tempo: 'operate at any tempo in the range 40 to 240 BPM; the default tempo for a new Project is 120 BPM'; 'the internal tempo clock only allows integer BPMs'; adjusted with Macro 1 in Tempo View; slaved: 'will synchronise to any external clock rates - including fractional values - in the range 30 to 300 BPM.' Exact match.
User Guide Tap Tempo: 'Hold down Shift and tap the Tempo/Swing button... You need at least three taps... and it will then calculate the BPM by averaging the last five taps.' Exact match.
User Guide Swing: 'Swing is adjusted with Macro 2, while in Tempo View'; 'from its default value of 50 (the range is 20 to 80) alters the timing of even-numbered steps (the off-beats).' Exact match.

User Guide Clock settings: 'When Clock Rx is OFF, the clock is in internal mode... Any external clock will be ignored. When Clock Rx is ON, Circuit Tracks is in AUTO mode and the BPM will be set by an externally applied MIDI clock at either the MIDI In or the USB ports if a valid one is applied; if this is not the case, Circuit Tracks will automatically switch to its internal clock.' Exact match.
User Guide: 'If Clock Tx is ON, Circuit Tracks is the clock master and its clock... will be available as MIDI Clock at the rear panel USB and MIDI Out connectors.' Exact match.

User Guide MIDI I/O: Pads 25-32 in four pairs. Pad 25/26 MIDI Note Rx/Tx (Green); 27/28 MIDI CC (Orange); 29/30 MIDI Program Change (Purple); 31/32 MIDI Clock (Pale Blue). 'By default, both MIDI Rx and MIDI Tx are ON... for all data categories.' Exact match including colours and pad numbers.
User Guide External clock: 'If an external clock is removed (or goes out of range), Circuit Tracks will stop playing. SYN remains displayed until Play is pressed.' Exact match.

User Guide: Sync Out is a 3.5mm TRS jack 'supplying a clock signal of 5 V amplitude'; Analogue clock rates selectable 1, 2, 4, 8 or 24 ppqn (pads 17-21), default 2 ppqn; 'Note that Swing (if set to something other than 50%) is not applied to the analogue clock output.' Exact match.

Ableton Link appears nowhere in the official User Guide; the guide exhaustively documents sync as MIDI clock (Rx/Tx, master/slave) via USB or DIN plus the analogue Sync Out. SoS review mentions the analogue sync and MIDI but no Link. Confirmed by comprehensive official documentation of sync methods and total absence of any Link feature (CT has no network/WiFi).

Programmer's Reference, Patch and Project Select table: Channel 16, PGM 0-63 = 'select project (instant)'; PGM 64-127 = 'select project (queued)'. Queued Project change takes effect at the end of the current pattern (per User Guide project-switching behaviour), i.e. a pattern boundary. Confirmed.

User Guide pattern switching: 'The default is for the current Pattern to play to its end before the new Pattern starts... the pad for the next Pattern will flash quickly while it is being queued... if you hold down Shift while selecting the next Pattern memory, it will start playing immediately from the corresponding Pattern step... the second Pattern will start playing from Step 12' (example continuing at equivalent step). Exact match.

User Guide Scenes: 'The two lower rows of pads in Mixer View represent the 16 Scenes'; each stores assigned pattern chains; Queuing Scenes: 'The pad for a queued Scene flashes green and at the end of the Drum 1 Pattern currently playing, the new Scene will begin playing from the start without losing sync.' Exact match.

User Guide Mixer View (button 11): 'The illuminated pads on Row 1 are Mute buttons for each track... The pad's illumination dims to indicate a Mute state.' No Solo function exists anywhere in the guide. Confirmed.

User Guide 'Performing with Muted Tracks': 'When a track is muted, its sequencer step pads become inactive. However, they then become available to play synth notes or chords, or drum hits, in real time.' Exact match.

User Guide 'The Filter Knob': entire output ('the sum of the sounds from all six internal tracks plus the two external audio inputs') fed through 'a traditional DJ-style filter'; centre detent = no filtering (LED dim white); anticlockwise = low-pass, clockwise = high-pass. Exact match.
User Guide FX Section (button 12): 'Sixteen delay and eight reverb presets are provided'; per-track send levels via Macro controls; 'delay time is related to the BPM.' Exact match.

User Guide Scales View (button 9): 16 scales, root note; 'Scale and Root Note - can both be altered during playback.' Changing scale: notes not in new scale get an 'intelligent decision... normally either one semitone above or below the original note. Changing scale in this way is not destructive.' Exact match.

User Guide: 'Every time you press Play, the Pattern restarts from Step 1... If you want to continue the Pattern from the point at which the sequencer was last stopped, press Shift and Play together.' Exact match.

User Guide Click track: 'activated or deactivated by holding Shift and pressing Clear... a metronome tick at every quarter note on all audio outputs.' Volume: 'press Tempo/Swing and use Macro 5.' Exact match.

User Guide Rear view: 'Outputs - L/Mono and R - Circuit Tracks' main audio outputs on two 1/4 inch TS jack sockets. Max. output level is +5.3 dBu (+/-1.5 dBu). With no plug in the R socket, the L/Mono socket carries a mono mix of L and R channels.' Exact match.

User Guide Rear view: USB-C port is DC power input, MIDI class compliant (transmit/receive MIDI), and used for firmware updates; 'NOTE - Circuit Tracks' USB port does not carry audio.' Audio must come from the 1/4 inch jacks. Exact match.

User Guide Advanced Setup View (entered 'holding down Shift while powering the unit on'): MIDI Thru config via Duplicate button - 'When Duplicate is lit bright green, the MIDI Thru port will act as a second MIDI Out'; useful for 'two pieces of hardware... that don't have MIDI thru ports themselves.' Exact match.
Programmer's Reference, Project Control ('Send messages on MIDI Channel 16'), Master Filter: frequency CC 74, '0-63=Low Pass, 64=OFF, 65-127=High Pass'; resonance CC 71 (default 30). Exact match.

Sound on Sound review states verbatim: 'Unfortunately there's no focus mode or master channel to save you from fiddling with channels, but as the Launchpad Pro has this I'm hopeful it will be added later.' Each track responds to a different MIDI channel. Confirmed as a stated workflow limitation.

verification.overallNotes:
All 33 claims CONFIRMED against official Circuit Tracks documentation. Primary sources verified and downloaded: (1) official Circuit Tracks User Guide v3 (fael-downloads-prod.focusrite.com/.../circuit_tracks_user_guide_v3_en.pdf, 5230 lines extracted), (2) official Circuit Tracks Programmer's Reference Guide v3 (same host, 1619 lines), (3) Sound on Sound review. All three are unambiguously about Circuit Tracks (rear panel shows two MIDI tracks, two audio inputs, USB-C, analogue Sync Out — all Tracks-specific; not the original Circuit or Rhythm). No cross-device contamination found; every cited fact was located in Tracks-specific material.

IMPORTANT FACTS THIS ANGLE MISSED (all from the official Tracks docs, worth adding to a performance/MIDI guide):

1. External MIDI control of the synths: A connected MIDI controller/DAW can play the two synth tracks live (Synth 1 = Ch1, Synth 2 = Ch2 by default). When played from an external controller, Circuit Tracks 'snaps' incoming notes to the nearest note in the current Scale. MIDI note Receive is ON by default.

2. Full synth-engine and FX control over MIDI: The Programmer's Reference documents extensive per-synth CC/NRPN control (oscillators, filter, envelopes, LFOs) plus project-level Reverb/Delay/Sidechain control on Channel 16 via CC and NRPN. This is a major MIDI-performance capability the claims omit entirely.

3. External patch selection: Program Change on Ch1 (0-63) selects Synth 1 patches, Ch2 (0-63) selects Synth 2 patches — distinct from the Ch16 project-change PGM covered in claim 19.

4. Advanced Setup View holds more than the MIDI Thru toggle: also Easy Start Tool (Mass Storage) on/off via Note button, Master Compressor on/off via FX button, and Save Lock (hold Shift+Save at power-on to prevent accidental overwrites during a live set). NOTE: the guide is internally inconsistent about the Master Compressor — the Setup View intro lists 'master compressor on/off' as a Setup View item, while the Master Compressor section places it in Advanced Setup View (FX button). Worth flagging in the guide.

5. Sidechain/ducking: External audio inputs (represented by the MIDI tracks) and both synths can be sidechained/ducked by any of the four drum tracks (Side Chain View, NRPN-controllable). Relevant to the 'route external synth audio back in' workflow in claim 9.

6. Pattern length and rate: Patterns are 16 or 32 steps (Step Page button); per-track Pattern play-rate is a BPM multiplier/divider with sync rates 1/4, 1/4T, 1/8, 1/8T, 1/16, 1/16T, 1/32, 1/32T. These affect performance timing of external gear.

7. Sticky Shift (enable in Setup View by pressing Shift) lets Shift-combos be used without holding Shift — a live-performance ergonomics feature.

8. View Lock and momentary long-press: most view buttons can be long-pressed to momentarily peek at a view (e.g., long-press Tempo/Swing to check BPM without switching views) — useful during performance.

9. Reverb has 8 presets (Small Chamber → Large Hall long reflection) and Delay 16 presets (Slapback Fast → 4th Triplets Ping Pong Wide) — full preset lists are in the guide if the reference guide wants tables. All delay times are BPM-locked.

10. Metronome/click and analogue-sync-rate settings are global (persist across Pack/Project changes); click on/off is NOT saved at power-off but click level IS.

verdicts.note: User Guide 'The MIDI Tracks' section: 'The two MIDI tracks behave just like the Synth tracks and support all the same sequencer functions... They do not control an internal synth engine but are intended to control external equipment or software via MIDI.' Tracks-specific feature (original 2015 Circuit had no MIDI tracks).
User Guide 'MIDI Channels' table: Synth 1=1, Synth 2=2, MIDI 1=3, MIDI 2=4, Drums 1-4=10. Exact match.

User Guide: 'Each track... can be set to any of MIDI channels 1-15. Channel 16 is reserved for the Project... No two tracks can transmit on the same MIDI channel.' Programmer's Reference confirms Project Control CCs and PGM are sent on Channel 16.

User Guide Setup View: 'It is entered by holding down Shift and pressing Save, and exited by pressing Play.' Settings include MIDI channel assignment, MIDI I/O (clock Rx/Tx), external analogue clock rate (also master compressor on/off and brightness).

User Guide 'Selecting Templates': 'Select a MIDI track and press Preset to access Template View. Eight MIDI templates are stored... represented by the top row of blue pads... Select a new template by pressing a pad; it lights white.'
User Guide: 'Each track can use a different template' and 'The eight default MIDI templates are identical.' Default CC table: Macro 1-8 = CC1, CC2, CC5, CC11, CC12, CC13, CC71, CC74. Exact match.

User Guide: templates 'edited and backed up in Components Editor.' Start/end values set anti-clockwise/clockwise limits. Polarity: 'This only affects the response of the LED below the control and does not affect the transmitted messages.'
User Guide: 'automated MIDI template controls will send data to the MIDI Out DIN port as well as the USB port, unlike parameters on the other tracks, which are only sent to the USB port.'

User Guide: on MIDI tracks the Mixer/FX Macro knobs 'act on the analogue audio signal applied at the two audio Inputs on the rear panel.' Recommended workflow: send MIDI to external synth, route its audio back into the inputs.

User Guide: 'range 40 to 240 BPM; the default tempo for a new Project is 120 BPM'; 'internal tempo clock only allows integer BPMs'; Macro 1 adjusts tempo in Tempo View; external clock synchronises to fractional rates 30 to 300 BPM.
User Guide Tap Tempo: 'Hold down Shift and tap the Tempo/Swing button... You need at least three taps... it will then calculate the BPM by averaging the last five taps.'
User Guide: 'Swing is adjusted with Macro 2, while in Tempo View'; 'default value of 50 (the range is 20 to 80)'; alters timing of even-numbered (off-beat) steps.

User Guide Clock settings: 'When Clock Rx is OFF, the clock is in internal mode... When Clock Rx is ON, Circuit Tracks is in AUTO mode and the BPM will be set by an externally applied MIDI clock at either the MIDI In or the USB ports... if this is not the case... will automatically switch to its internal clock.'
User Guide: 'If Clock Tx is ON, Circuit Tracks is the clock master and its clock... will be available as MIDI Clock at the rear panel USB and MIDI Out connectors.'

User Guide MIDI I/O table: Pads 25-32 as four Rx/Tx pairs - Note (green), CC (orange), Program Change (purple), Clock (pale blue). 'By default, both MIDI Rx and MIDI Tx are ON... for all data categories.'
User Guide: 'If an external clock is removed (or goes out of range), Circuit Tracks will stop playing. SYN remains displayed until Play is pressed.'
User Guide Analogue clock rates: Sync Out at 5 V amplitude; rate selectable 1, 2, 4, 8 or 24 ppqn (pads 17-21), default 2 ppqn; 'Swing... is not applied to the analogue clock output.'

Neither the User Guide nor the Sound on Sound review mentions Ableton Link; both describe sync only via MIDI clock (master/slave) and the analogue Sync Out. Circuit Tracks has no Ableton Link. Confirmed by absence in both official/primary sources plus device knowledge.

Programmer's Reference 'Patch and Project Select' table, Channel 16 PGM: value 0-63 = 'select project (instant)', 64-127 = 'select project (queued)'. Matches (queued = takes effect at pattern boundary).

User Guide: 'if you select a Pattern while another is already playing... The default is for the current Pattern to play to its end... the pad for the next Pattern will flash quickly while it is being queued... if you hold down Shift while selecting the next Pattern memory, it will start playing immediately from the corresponding Pattern step.'

User Guide: 'The two lower rows of pads in Mixer View represent the 16 Scenes.' Queuing Scenes: 'The pad for a queued Scene flashes green and at the end of the Drum 1 Pattern currently playing, the new Scene will begin playing from the start without losing sync.'

User Guide: 'The illuminated pads on Row 1 are Mute buttons for each track... The pad's illumination dims to indicate a Mute state.' Mixer is button 11. No Solo function documented anywhere in the guide.

User Guide 'Performing with Muted Tracks': 'When a track is muted, its sequencer step pads become inactive. However, they then become available to play synth notes or chords, or drum hits, in real time.'

User Guide 'The Filter Knob': entire output including the two external audio inputs fed through a DJ-style filter; 'controls a low-pass filter when you turn it anticlockwise from the centre position and a high-pass filter when you turn it clockwise'; centre detent = no filtering.
User Guide FX View (button 12): 'Sixteen delay and eight reverb presets are provided.' Send levels per-track via Macros. 'In all cases, delay time is related to the BPM.'

User Guide: 16 scales in Scales View (button 9); scale and root note 'can both be altered during playback'; changing keynote transposes non-destructively; on a scale change, out-of-scale notes are substituted 'either one semitone above or below the original note' and it 'is not destructive.' Minor: substitution occurs on scale change specifically, transposition on keynote change - the claim conflates these slightly but substance is correct. Note also scale/root applies to both Synth AND both MIDI tracks.

User Guide: 'Every time you press Play, the Pattern restarts from Step 1... If you want to continue the Pattern from the point at which the sequencer was last stopped, press Shift and Play together.' Minor framing: Play is a stop/start toggle, so the restart happens on the next Play press after stopping, not literally 'while running', but the default-vs-Shift behaviour is exactly as claimed.
User Guide Click track: 'activated or deactivated by holding Shift and pressing Clear... metronome tick at every quarter note on all audio outputs.' Level via Macro 5 in Tempo View.

User Guide rear panel: 'main audio outputs on two 1/4" TS jack sockets. Max. output level is +5.3 dBu (+/-1.5 dBu). With no plug in the R socket, the L/Mono socket carries a mono mix of L and R channels.'

User Guide: USB-C is 'also the DC power input... connect to computers to interface with Novation Components... transmit and receive MIDI... Also used for firmware updates. NOTE - Circuit Tracks' USB port does not carry audio.'

User Guide Advanced Setup View (entered by 'holding down Shift while powering the unit on'): MIDI Thru port can 'duplicate the output of the MIDI Out port. This is useful if you have two pieces of hardware... that don't have MIDI thru ports themselves.' Set via the Duplicate button.
Programmer's Reference 'Project Control' (Channel 16): Master Filter frequency = CC74, '0-63=Low Pass, 64=OFF, 65-127=High Pass'; resonance = CC71. Exact match.

Sound on Sound review states verbatim: 'Unfortunately there's no focus mode or master channel to save you from fiddling with channels, but as the Launchpad Pro has this I'm hopeful it will be added later.'

verification.overallNotes:
All 33 claims verified as CONFIRMED against primary sources: the official Circuit Tracks User Guide v3 and Programmer's Reference Guide v3 (text extracted locally from the downloaded PDFs via pdftotext, since WebFetch returned only binary), plus the Sound on Sound review. NO cross-device contamination found - every claim is genuinely Circuit Tracks-specific. In particular the two dedicated MIDI tracks (MIDI 1 / MIDI 2), the 8 per-MIDI-track Components templates, the analogue-audio-input Macro mapping on MIDI tracks, and channel-16 Project control are all Tracks features that do NOT exist on the original 2015 Novation Circuit.

Two claims have minor framing imprecision (verdict still confirmed): (26) the ~semitone note substitution happens specifically when you change the SCALE, while changing the ROOT NOTE/keynote transposes - the claim slightly merges the two, but both are non-destructive as stated; (27) Play is a stop/start toggle, so 'pressing Play while running' literally stops it - the described default (restart from Step 1) vs Shift+Play (continue from stopped step) behavior is exactly correct for the subsequent Play press.

Facts MISSED for the midi-performance angle that are worth capturing:
- Scale and root note apply to BOTH synth tracks AND BOTH MIDI tracks, so sequenced/played MIDI notes to external gear are constrained/transposed by the selected scale (User Guide, Scales section) - directly relevant to MIDI performance.
- When a new MIDI template is selected, existing recorded automation is NOT erased; it is re-applied to whatever new CC the Macro now sends (User Guide, Selecting Templates).
- MIDI tracks support the full sequencer feature set: velocity, gate, probability, micro steps, Mutate, pattern length/sync-rate, and Note-View live playing from the performance pads.
- External audio inputs (fed by the MIDI tracks' returned synth audio) can be side-chain 'ducked' by the drum tracks and are routed through the FX/Mixer sections.
- Advanced Setup View (Shift + power-on) also toggles the Easy Start / Mass Storage mode (Note button) and the Master Compressor (FX 12), in addition to MIDI Thru duplicate mode.
- Under external clock, turning Macro 1 displays 'SYN' in red and does not change tempo; the saved Project BPM returns when Play is pressed after clock loss.
- The Programmer's Reference documents a full Channel-16 Project Control CC map (tempo, swing, filter freq/res, FX levels, mutate, etc.) and per-synth NRPN parameter access - a richer external-control surface than the claims capture.

verdicts.note: User Guide 'The MIDI Tracks' section: 'The two MIDI tracks behave just like the Synth tracks and support all the same sequencer functions... They do not control an internal synth engine but are intended to control external equipment or software via MIDI.' Two dedicated MIDI tracks (MIDI 1, MIDI 2), no internal sound engine. Confirmed.
User Guide Setup View / MIDI Channels table: 'Synth 1 = 1, Synth 2 = 2, MIDI 1 = 3, MIDI 2 = 4, Drums 1-4 = 10' with note 'all four drum tracks use the same MIDI channel.' Exact match.

User Guide: 'Each track... can be set to any of MIDI channels 1-15. Channel 16 is reserved for the Project' and 'Important: No two tracks can transmit on the same MIDI channel.' Programmer's Reference confirms Ch16 carries the Project-level CCs (reverb/delay sends, master filter, sidechain via 'Send messages on MIDI Channel 16') and project Program Change, so the 'global project CCs and project program-change' enrichment is correct.

User Guide Setup View: 'entered by holding down Shift and pressing Save, and exited by pressing Play.' Setup View includes MIDI channel assignment, MIDI I/O (clock Rx/Tx), external analogue clock rate (ppqn), plus master compressor and brightness. Exact match.

User Guide 'Selecting Templates': 'Select a MIDI track and press Preset (button 14) to access Template View. Eight MIDI templates are stored within Circuit Tracks, represented by the top row of blue pads... Select a new template by pressing a pad; it lights white.' Exact match; Preset is button 14.

User Guide: 'Each track can use a different template' and 'The eight default MIDI templates are identical.' Default CC table maps Macro 1-8 to CC1, CC2, CC5, CC11, CC12, CC13, CC71, CC74 respectively. Exact match.

User Guide: templates 'can be edited and backed up in Components Editor'; you set start value (fully anti-clockwise) and end value (fully clockwise); polarity unipolar/bipolar 'only affects the response of the LED below the control and does not affect the transmitted messages.' Exact match. Minor nuance: templates are the 8 pre-stored slots edited in Components rather than 'created' from nothing, but that is consistent with the claim's intent.

User Guide: 'automated MIDI template controls will send data to the MIDI Out DIN port as well as the USB port, unlike parameters on the other tracks, which are only sent to the USB port.' Exact match.

User Guide: on MIDI tracks the Macro knobs in Mixer/FX View 'act on the analogue audio signal applied at the two audio Inputs on the rear panel.' Recommended workflow: 'send MIDI data from Circuit Tracks to your external synth or drum machine, while routing the audio outputs of that device back into Circuit Tracks' audio inputs.' Exact match.

User Guide Tempo: 'range 40 to 240 BPM; the default tempo for a new Project is 120 BPM'; 'Macro control 1 is used to adjust the tempo.' Also: 'the internal tempo clock only allows integer BPMs... Circuit Tracks will synchronise to any external clock rates - including fractional values - in the range 30 to 300 BPM.' Exact match.

User Guide Tap Tempo: 'Hold down Shift and tap the Tempo/Swing button... You need at least three taps for Circuit Tracks to change its tempo setting... it will then calculate the BPM by averaging the last five taps.' Exact match.

User Guide: 'Swing is adjusted with Macro 2, while in Tempo View'; 'Altering the Swing parameter from its default value of 50 (the range is 20 to 80) alters the timing of even-numbered steps (the off-beats).' Exact match.

User Guide Clock settings: 'When Clock Rx is OFF, the clock is in internal mode... Any external clock will be ignored. When Clock Rx is ON, Circuit Tracks is in AUTO mode and the BPM will be set by an externally applied MIDI clock at either the MIDI In or the USB ports if a valid one is applied; if this is not the case, Circuit Tracks will automatically switch to its internal clock.' Exact match.
User Guide: 'If Clock Tx is ON, Circuit Tracks is the clock master and its clock... will be available as MIDI Clock at the rear panel USB and MIDI Out connectors.' Exact match.

User Guide MIDI I/O: Pads 25-32 as four Rx/Tx pairs - Note (green, 25/26), CC (orange, 27/28), Program Change (purple, 29/30), Clock (pale blue, 31/32). 'By default, both MIDI Rx and MIDI Tx are ON... for all data categories.' Exact match.
User Guide External clock: 'If an external clock is removed (or goes out of range), Circuit Tracks will stop playing. SYN remains displayed until Play is pressed.' Exact match.

User Guide Analogue clock rates: Sync Out at 5V amplitude; rate selectable 1, 2, 4, 8 or 24 ppqn (pads 17-21), default 2 ppqn. 'Note that Swing (if set to something other than 50%) is not applied to the analogue clock output.' Exact match.

Negative claim, supported by absence. Neither the User Guide nor the Programmer's Reference mentions Ableton Link anywhere; documented sync is exclusively MIDI clock (master via Clock Tx / slave via Clock Rx AUTO) and the analogue Sync Out. The Sound on Sound review likewise mentions only analogue sync and MIDI, never Link. Circuit Tracks has no network/WiFi hardware, which Link requires, so native Link is not possible. Confirmed.

Programmer's Reference, Patch and Project Select table (Ch 16): PGM value 0-63 = 'select project (instant)', value 64-127 = 'select project (queued)'. The queued behaviour aligns with the User Guide's pattern/project boundary queuing. Confirmed.

User Guide pattern switching: default queues next pattern to end of current one and 'the pad for the next Pattern will flash quickly while it is being queued'; 'if you hold down Shift while selecting the next Pattern memory, it will start playing immediately from the corresponding Pattern step' (worked example: current at Step 11 -> new pattern starts at Step 12). Exact match.

User Guide Scenes: 'The two lower rows of pads in Mixer View represent the 16 Scenes.' Each scene stores the playing patterns/chains. Queuing: 'The pad for a queued Scene flashes green and at the end of the Drum 1 Pattern currently playing, the new Scene will begin playing from the start without losing sync.' Exact match.

User Guide Mixer View (button 11): 'The illuminated pads on Row 1 are Mute buttons for each track... The pad's illumination dims to indicate a Mute state.' No Solo function appears anywhere in the User Guide (grep for 'solo' returns nothing). Confirmed.

User Guide 'Performing with Muted Tracks': 'When a track is muted, its sequencer step pads become inactive. However, they then become available to play synth notes or chords, or drum hits, in real time.' For drums this uses Velocity/Gate View. Confirmed.

User Guide 'The Filter Knob': 'The entire audio output of Circuit Tracks - the sum of the sounds from all six internal tracks plus the two external audio inputs - is fed through a traditional DJ-style filter'; 'controls a low-pass filter when you turn it anticlockwise from the centre position and a high-pass filter when you turn it clockwise'; 'the control has a detent in the centre - in this position, no filtering takes place.' Exact match.

User Guide FX Section: 'Sixteen delay and eight reverb presets are provided.' The 16 delay presets are all tempo-relative note divisions (e.g. '16 cycles per bar'), i.e. synced to project BPM. Per-track sends set by Macro knobs. Exact match.

User Guide Scales View (button 9): 'selection of one of sixteen different music scales' (full 16-scale table, pads 17-32). Both scale and root note 'can both be altered during playback.' Changing keynote transposes notes; changing scale where notes fall out of scale: 'Circuit Tracks makes an intelligent decision as to which note to play instead, which will normally be either one semitone above or below the original note. Changing scale in this way is not [destructive].' Confirmed.

User Guide: 'Every time you press Play, the Pattern restarts from Step 1 (or the chosen Start point). If you want to continue the Pattern from the point at which the sequencer was last stopped, press Shift and Play together.' Exact match.

User Guide Click track: 'The Click (or metronome) can be activated or deactivated by holding Shift and pressing Clear... you will hear a metronome tick at every quarter note on all audio outputs.' 'To adjust the volume of the Click, press Tempo/Swing and use Macro 5.' Exact match.

User Guide rear-panel: 'Outputs - L/Mono and R - Circuit Tracks' main audio outputs on two 1/4-inch TS jack sockets. Max. output level is +5.3 dBu (+/-1.5 dBu). With no plug in the R socket, the L/Mono socket carries a mono mix of L and R channels.' Exact match.

User Guide: USB-C port 'is also the DC power input... allows devices supporting MIDI via USB to transmit and receive MIDI data. Also used for firmware updates. NOTE - Circuit Tracks' USB port does not carry audio.' Audio must therefore come from the 1/4-inch jacks. Confirmed.

User Guide Advanced Setup View (entered by 'holding down Shift while powering the unit on'): MIDI Thru can 'duplicate the output of the MIDI Out port... useful if you have two pieces of hardware that you wish to control with the MIDI tracks that don't have MIDI thru ports themselves. Use the Duplicate button to set the behaviour. When Duplicate is lit bright green, the MIDI Thru port will act as a second MIDI Out.' Exact match.

Programmer's Reference, Project Control (Ch 16), Master Filter: frequency = CC74, '0-63=Low Pass, 64=OFF, 65-127=High Pass'; resonance = CC71. Header states 'Send messages on MIDI Channel 16.' Exact match (resonance default value is 30, immaterial to the claim).

Sound on Sound review, verbatim: 'Each track responds to a different MIDI channel. Unfortunately there's no focus mode or master channel to save you from fiddling with channels, but as the Launchpad Pro has this I'm hopeful it will be added later.' Exact match.

verification.overallNotes:
All 33 midi-performance claims independently verified and CONFIRMED against the two official Circuit Tracks documents (User Guide v3 and Programmer's Reference v3, both extracted from the source PDFs) and the Sound on Sound review. No refutations, no wrong-device contamination — every fact was traced to text explicitly about Circuit Tracks (not the original Circuit or Circuit Rhythm). The PDFs would not render via WebFetch (binary), so I downloaded them and extracted text with pdftotext -layout; source excerpts are quoted in each note.

Minor nuances worth flagging (do not change any verdict):
- Claim 7: the device does not let you 'create' templates from scratch on the hardware; there are exactly 8 pre-stored template slots that you edit/back up in Components. The claim's wording is fine in intent.
- Claim 18 is a negative/absence claim; it is well-supported (no Link mention anywhere in official docs, no network hardware) but rests on absence of evidence rather than a positive statement.

Important midi-performance facts the claim set MISSED entirely (candidates to add to the guide):
1. Program Change on the synth channels drives PATCH selection, not just projects: Programmer's Reference — PGM on Ch 1 selects Synth 1 patch (0-63), PGM on Ch 2 selects Synth 2 patch (0-63); drum patch selection is via Note messages (separate Drum Control table). The User Guide's 'External Patch Select' covers this too.
2. Full Project-level (Ch 16) CC/NRPN map beyond the master filter: Reverb sends CC88/89 (synths), CC90/106/109/110 (drums); Delay sends CC111-116; reverb type/decay/damping and delay time/sync/feedback/width/ratio/slew are NRPNs (1:6..1:20); sidechain sources are NRPNs (2:55 etc). This is the real substance of 'global project CCs on Ch 16.'
3. Side Chain: 7 presets per synth/MIDI-input track, OFF by default on both synths and both MIDI tracks; Side Chain View is the secondary view of the FX button (Shift+FX), with page 1 = synths and page 2 = MIDI/Audio-In tracks, navigated by the J/K buttons.
4. Delay preset table is fully tempo-synced note divisions (Slapback, 32nd/16th/8th/4th, triplets, dotted, ping-pong, swung variants) — relevant to 'delay synced to BPM.'
5. MIDI tracks' note range: transposable up to 5 octaves up / 6 octaves down when entering notes (per hardware overview); scale + root note apply to BOTH synth tracks AND both MIDI tracks simultaneously.
6. External MIDI controllers can drive the Macros and that automation can be recorded exactly like knob moves (requires CC Rx ON, the default).
7. Duplicate can copy a Pattern between the two synth tracks, between a synth and a MIDI track, or between drum tracks — but NOT between a synth/MIDI track and a drum track.
8. Sticky Shift is an option toggled in Setup View (press Shift while in Setup View); grid brightness is Pad 24 in Setup View (dims ~50%, saved across power cycles).
9. Master compressor is on by default and toggled by FX (button 12) in Advanced Setup View (the User Guide also refers to it as 'the FX button in Setup View' on p102 — a minor internal doc inconsistency, but the operative control is FX in Advanced Setup View).
10. When a new MIDI template is selected, existing recorded automation is NOT erased and is re-applied to whatever new CC the Macro now sends — a subtle performance gotcha.
